Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper explicitly identifies the candidate gene as NCBI accession Loc_Os01g65670 and lists three full-length cDNAs: AK121636, AK065286, and AK119529.

Applied_Species.reason: The study is conducted in rice throughout, including NILs and transgenic rice plants.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper explicitly names the species as Oryza sativa L.; both indica and japonica are described as subspecies of O. sativa.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: NYZ, ZH11, ZS97, NIL(ZS97), and NIL(NYZ) are all explicitly used in mapping and transformation experiments.

Source_Species.reason: The OsAAP6 alleles used for cloning/complementation/overexpression derive from rice.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The donor varieties ZS97 and NYZ are rice, explicitly described as Oryza sativa.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: ZS97 is the high-GPC parent/allele source and NYZ the low-GPC parent/allele source in the mapping population and allele comparison.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper states qPC1 encodes a putative amino acid transporter belonging to the APC superfamily, has a PF01490 consensus domain, and is highly homologous to the AAP family; it corresponds to OsAAP6.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are directly reported in Results/Methods: map-based cloning/fine mapping, overexpression, complementation, RNAi, co-segregation, qRT-PCR-expression correlation with phenotype, GUS promoter assays, amino-acid uptake/distribution assays, metabolite profiling, TEM, and subcellular localization in protoplasts.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper sequenced OsAAP6 promoter/5'-UTR/coding-region polymorphisms in 197 rice mini-core accessions. No public accession number is given, so 'accession number NA' is consistent with the paper's lack of such a number.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results explicitly state transcripts were detected in all examined tissues, most abundant in endosperms at 5–12 DAF; GUS was strong in vascular tissues of hull, root, pulvinus, internode, node, seed and endosperm; histochemistry showed expression mainly in root rhizodermis and phloem.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper reports significantly higher OsAAP6 transcript levels in NIL(ZS97) than NIL(NYZ) endosperm, and in the mini-core analysis expression was assayed at 5 DAF with higher-expression haplotypes linked to higher GPC. The ZS97-vs-NYZ difference during 5–12 DAF is directly supported by Fig. 2a.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: OsAAP6-GFP completely overlapped with the ER marker BiP-RFP and not with SCAMP1-RFP; the paper concludes OsAAP6 is localized on the ER.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The Results directly link OsAAP6 to grain protein content, storage proteins (glutelins, prolamins, globulins, albumins), and total amino acid content in grains.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These terminal products are proteins and amino acids, both directly measured/reported.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The study measures grain/endosperm traits, including developing endosperm expression, protein bodies in developing endosperm, and grain storage protein accumulation in seeds.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper repeatedly states that increased GPC and amino acids improve grain nutritional quality and discusses cereals as dietary protein sources for humans.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results and Table 1 support that OsAAP6 is a positive regulator of GPC/nutritional quality, increasing storage proteins and total amino acids; Table 1 also shows increased amylose and reduced starch and gel consistency in high-OsAAP6 genotypes.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Table 1 explicitly states data are averages of 2 years of field trials; Methods describe amino-acid uptake assays in sterile culture with a 20-amino-acid mixture; developmental stages 5–12 DAF and 10 DAF are repeatedly analyzed.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The OX construct uses the CaMV 35S promoter; complementation uses the native 1.8-kb OsAAP6 promoter plus 5'-UTR from ZS97; GUS assays use OsAAP6 promoter/5'-UTR deletions from ZS97 and NYZ.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper supports that OsAAP6 could improve GPC/nutritional quality without detectable effects on morphology/flowering time/yield, and notes value for genetic improvement. However, 'useful allele selection or promoter engineering/editing of cis-regulatory variants' is an extrapolation not directly tested.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Table 1 shows higher protein is associated with higher amylose, lower starch, and reduced gel consistency; the paper also states no effect on plant morphology, flowering time, or grain yield in this study.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Each component is reported: OsAAP6 is cloned qPC1; major variation is in promoter/5'-UTR with only a synonymous coding change noted between parents; two common 5'-UTR cis-element variants seem associated with GPC diversity mainly in indica; OsAAP6 affects amino-acid uptake/distribution and enlarges protein bodies; it is presented as a transporter/regulator rather than biosynthetic enzyme or transcription factor.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All six summarized points are directly supported by Results: fine mapping to a 6.7-kb interval with only OsAAP6; gain/loss-of-function effects on GPC/storage proteins/total amino acids; positive expression-GPC correlation; expression in developing endosperm and vascular/root tissues with ER localization; promoter/5'-UTR polymorphism association mainly in indica; altered amino-acid uptake/distribution, gene expression, and protein body size.
